subtasks.description: Query sam_licenses for current Adobe contracted state: product line, quantity, unit cost, contract end date, and any auto-renewal flags. Filter for Adobe vendor.
Query consumption_facts for Q4 2024 actual spend and 90-day usage trend by Adobe product line. Include metrics: total Q4 spend, units consumed, trend direction (increasing/decreasing/flat).

Reconcile contracted state from sam_licenses with Q4 spend and 90-day usage trends from consumption_facts. For each Adobe product line, classify renewal posture (expand/hold/reduce) based on usage-to-contract ratio, spending trends, and consumption velocity. Generate renewal trajectory and product-line-specific recommendations.

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks: (1) sam_licenses is a single-table lookup for contract state → small sql_gen; (2) consumption_facts is a single-table aggregation by product line over time range → small sql_gen; (3) reconciliation across two data sources (sam state vs. actual consumption) with classification and recommendation generation → mid cross_recon. The cross_recon tier is mid rather than frontier because the logic, while requiring multi-signal reasoning, involves straightforward comparisons and standard classification (no complex inference or multi-document extraction needed). We do not add a separate policy_action because renewal posture classification is part of the reconciliation decision-making, not a formal governance policy evaluation.

output.sql: SELECT l.product, l.quantity, l.unit_cost, l.contract_end_date, l.auto_renewal_flag, c.event_type, SUM(c.amount_usd) as q4_spend, AVG(c.daily_active_seats) as avg_seats FROM consumption_facts c JOIN sam_licenses l ON c.license_id = l.license_id WHERE l.vendor_name = 'Adobe' AND c.event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND c.event_date <= '2024-12-31' GROUP BY l.product, l.quantity, l.unit_cost, l.contract_end_date, l.auto_renewal_flag, c.event_type ORDER BY l.product, q4_spend DESC

SELECT cf.product, SUM(cf.amount_usd) as q4_2024_spend, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) as units_consumed, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.event_date) as active_days, CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN cf.event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' THEN cf.daily_active_seats ELSE 0 END) > SUM(CASE WHEN cf.event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' AND cf.event_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' THEN cf.daily_active_seats ELSE 0 END) THEN 'increasing' WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN cf.event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' THEN cf.daily_active_seats ELSE 0 END) < SUM(CASE WHEN cf.event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' AND cf.event_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' THEN cf.daily_active_seats ELSE 0 END) THEN 'decreasing' ELSE 'flat' END as trend_direction FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher = 'Adobe' AND cf.event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND cf.event_date <= '2024-12-31' GROUP BY cf.product ORDER BY q4_2024_spend DESC
